Name: hardware_efficient_inference
Title: Optimizing Neural Network Inference on Commodity GPUs through Hardware-Aware Quantization and Kernel Fusion
Short Hypothesis: By combining hardware-aware quantization techniques with kernel fusion, we can significantly improve the efficiency of neural network inference on commodity GPUs, leading to faster and more accurate results.
Related Work: Recent works such as APNAS, HLQ, and SNIP have explored the idea of optimizing neural networks for efficient inference on GPUs. However, these works have focused primarily on quantization and mixed-precision training, without fully exploiting the potential of kernel fusion. Our proposal builds upon these works by integrating kernel fusion with hardware-aware quantization to achieve even greater efficiency gains.
Abstract: This project proposes a novel approach to optimizing neural network inference on commodity GPUs. By combining hardware-aware quantization techniques with kernel fusion, we aim to reduce the computational complexity and memory bandwidth requirements of neural networks, leading to faster and more accurate results. Our approach involves developing a framework that can automatically generate optimized kernel code for a given neural network, taking into account the specific hardware characteristics of the target GPU. We will evaluate our approach on a range of benchmark datasets and compare its performance to state-of-the-art methods.
Experiments: Evaluate the performance of our approach on benchmark datasets such as ImageNet and CIFAR-10
Compare the performance of our approach to state-of-the-art methods such as APNAS and SNIP
Investigate the impact of different quantization schemes and kernel fusion techniques on the performance of our approach
Conduct a detailed analysis of the computational complexity and memory bandwidth requirements of our approach
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the optimization problem may make it challenging to find the optimal solution
The approach may not be suitable for all types of neural networks or GPU architectures
The evaluation of the approach may be limited by the availability of benchmark datasets and computational resources
